stem: A food production study measures package weight for 20 samples to determine if the process variability is within ±5g of the nominal weight. The sample standard deviation is 2.88 grams (sample variance = 8.27). Estimate the true population standard deviation of package weight and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Sample SD = 2.88 grams. 95% CI for σ (chi-squared): [2.19, 4.2]. Point estimate: 2.88 grams.

common_failure_modes: Using z-based CI for the mean instead of χ²-based CI for the variance
Reporting the sample SD (2.88) as the population SD without uncertainty
Not recognizing that confidence intervals for variance are asymmetric
